What is a plan entry in Mise?

One planned item in a week's plan (plans/<week>.json). Fields: id (unique, the merge key), date (YYYY-MM-DD), slot (breakfast|lunch|dinner|smoothie|snack), exactly one of recipeId or freeText, servings, and optional flags: pinned (GENERATE WEEK must never clear or overwrite it), out (eating-out placeholder, always pinned, carries estCalories/estProtein as the assumed restaurant macros). Entries stack: several entries may share the same date+slot.

What does out: true mean on a plan entry?

The slot is an eating-out placeholder: a free lunch or restaurant dinner. It is always written with pinned: true and freeText "eating out", so re-rolls never clear or refill the slot and the shopping list ignores it. Its estCalories/estProtein carry the ASSUMED macros (the recipe pool's average for that meal type times a 0.85 undershoot), and dayTotals counts that credit so the rest of the day is planned around a realistic total.

Why is the eating-out estimate deliberately undershot?

You do not know the restaurant portion in advance. Crediting slightly low means the generator closes the small remaining gap with a snack you can simply skip if dinner ran big. The alternative, crediting high, would plan the day around calories that might never arrive.

What does pinned mean and when do I use it?

pinned: true protects a plan entry from GENERATE MY WEEK and RE-ROLL: the generator keeps it and never overwrites its slot. Use it when the user wants a specific meal locked to a specific day while the rest of the week regenerates around it.

What is plan.locked?

A whole-week flag set from the List tab's GOING TO THE STORE button. locked: true means the groceries for this week are already bought: GENERATE/RE-ROLL refuse to run and add/remove/move edits ask for confirmation first. It protects the plan from silently drifting away from food that has already been paid for.

What is plan.buffer?

The week's buffer snack: ONE batch-prepped recipe (usually 7 portions) chosen by GENERATE WEEK as the measured fridge stand-by for extra hunger. Its batch is added to the shopping list like a planned entry, and portions eaten are tallied per day on the Cook view into the daily log's buffer count. It is display-and-shopping only; it never feeds plan dayTotals.

How does a recipe's JSON describe ingredients?

ingredients is an array of {qty, unit, food, staple, optional note and optional flags}. staple: true means the household always has it, so it never lands on the shopping list. optional: true ingredients are skipped by the diet classifier and the avoid-ingredient screen. Quantities are for the WHOLE recipe at its stated servings; per-serving amounts are qty divided by servings.

How is a recipe's nutrition stored?

nutrition = {calories, protein, carbs, fat, method} PER SERVING. method is "estimated" (computed from ingredient tables) or "book-verified" (audited against the Greger books). When ingredients change materially, the old numbers no longer hold: keep method "estimated" and say the macros need re-estimation rather than inventing new values.

What are the Daily Dozen food groups a recipe can contribute?

foodGroups carries servings per recipe-serving for: beans, berries, otherFruit, cruciferousVeg, greens, otherVeg, flaxseed, nuts, spicesHerbs, wholeGrains, beverages, plus a method key (metadata, not a group). The week generator uses these to close Daily Dozen gaps; beverages, otherFruit and otherVeg are largely daily habits tallied on Home rather than recipe-delivered.

How does the shopping list get built?

Derived, never hand-maintained: aggregate the week's plan entries (recipeId entries only; freeText and eating-out placeholders are ignored), scale each recipe's ingredients by servings/recipe.servings, merge duplicates by food+unit, subtract pantry staples that are onHand and not runningLow, add runningLow staples back, add the buffer snack's batch, then round quantities up to purchasable amounts. Check-state and manual items survive rebuilds.

What are pantry perishables and how does expiry work?

pantry.perishables are dated fresh items ({food, added, optional useSoon}). Each food class has a shelf life (fish ~3d, poultry/meat ~4d, leafy greens ~6d, sturdy veg/tofu ~8d, dairy ~10d, roots ~18d, eggs/hard cheese ~28d, default 14d). Items past added+shelfLife auto-expire out of the pantry; items within their last 3 days are auto-flagged useSoon, which makes the week generator favor recipes that cook them and shows a USE SOON strip on the Cook tab.

Which profile's recipes does the assistant see?

A profile's pool = the shared bank (root recipes/, filtered by the profile's phase, diet, and avoidIngredients) plus the profile's own scoped recipes, which override bank recipes with the same id. Edits made for a profile are saved as profile-scoped overrides; the shared bank is never mutated by an assistant edit.
